channel.title: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN
channel.description: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN RSS

item.title: Greater inspection of tobacco and similar products planned
item.description:
A public consultation is underway on the review of legislation relating to smoking, until April 8th. The measures proposed by the Government aim to illegalize the possession of electronic cigarettes in public spaces and expand public smoking-free areas. As preventive policies, the remaining measures aim at greater supervision of new products and a new packaging regime for products containing tobacco. In addition, the Executive is also considering equipping inspection agents with audio and image recording devices
PEDRO MILHEIRÃO The Government intends to ban the possession of electronic cigarettes in public spaces, according to the Health Services (SSM) public consultation document on the “Amendment to the Smoking Prevention and Control Regime”. Ownership will cover “all components of electronic cigarettes”, such as the device, the e-liquid and the capsule, among others. The consumption of these products in public spaces “will be considered [as] possession of them” and illegal, the document states. The SSM justify this measure by the fact that the acquisition of electronic cigarettes in neighboring regions is easy and the detection of these products is difficult. Since the electronic cigarette is “small in size, easy to hide” and leaves “no visible signs of consumption (…) the entire process is highly hidden and difficult to detect immediately”, explains the organization. It should be remembered that the sale of electronic cigarettes has been prohibited since January 1, 2018, and the manufacture, distribution, sale, import, export and transport of these cigarettes were banned on December 5, 2022, in order to “allow the consumption of these previously purchased products”. In recent years, in neighboring regions and countries, electronic cigarettes have served as a “means for transporting illicit substances”, such as “etomidate”, say the SSM. In Hong Kong, possession of electronic cigarettes in public places will be prohibited from April 30th this year. On another point, the authorities intend to expand the areas where smoking is prohibited outdoors, creating new prohibition zones “in certain streets or squares with a large concentration of people”, which will be designated by order from the Chief Executive. In these areas, smoking will only be permitted in “smoking areas”. Taking into account “the reality of Macau, where spaces on public roads are limited”, and “the minimization of impacts caused to non-smokers”, the SSM anticipate a “reasonable organization”. Furthermore, the Executive intends that “entrances and exits to schools, daycare centers, among other establishments, are integrated into places where smoking is prohibited, provided for in the ‘Tobacco Control Law’, in order to protect the health of students and children”. The objective is to “effectively resolve the issue of second-hand smoke in the entry and exit points of these establishments”, argue the SSM. In the first quarter of 2026, the SSM implemented an experimental smoking ban area in the streets surrounding Parque Dr. Carlos d’ Assumpção. In November last year, the measure prohibiting smoking in the entrances of several educational establishments was also implemented. Although the ban is already in force in certain places, namely bus stops, gardens, beaches and schools, “smoking is still permitted in open spaces, such as streets and squares, so the public eventually comes into contact with secondhand smoke in the outdoor environment”, state the SSM. According to the organization, “the public is particularly concerned about the act of smoking while walking” and “the harm caused by secondhand smoke at the entrances and exits” of schools and daycare centers.
Prevent consumption of new products As a preventive solution, the document also suggests a ban on the manufacture, distribution, marketing, import and export and transport to the interior or exterior of nicotine pouches, herbal cigarettes, tobacco or water pipe paste, hookahs and their components, as well as “similar products”. Although they are not yet widespread in Macau, the SSM anticipate that “these products may reach a significant scale of consumption”. Although water pipes are regulated by the “Tobacco Control Law”, which prohibits their consumption in closed public spaces, “there has been an increase in their popularity among young people globally”, since “they can be shared by several people at the same time”, which leads them to be “often consumed on social occasions outdoors”. On the other hand, the legislation in force still “does not cover nicotine pouches or herbal cigarettes that do not contain tobacco”. “In recent years, new types of tobacco, nicotine products and tobacco substitutes have constantly appeared on the market”, observe the SSM, “with the aim of attracting a greater number of new young consumers”. These new products are often advertised as “tobacco-free”, “harm-free” and “natural”, influencing young non-smokers. Taking into account that “tobacco product packaging constitutes the main means of information with which consumers have the greatest interaction”, the authorities intend to introduce a new standardized packaging regime for tobacco products, in line with the guidelines of the World Health Organization. This regime provides for “an increase in the area allocated to labels and images of health warnings on all tobacco product packaging”, to “85% of the surface of the main face of the packaging”, indicates the document. However, “to ensure an orderly transition without obstacles in the market, it is recommended that an additional period of one year be allocated, counting from the date of publication of the law, for the aforementioned provisions to enter into force after the end of that period”.
Inspection agents with portable cameras Another of the Government's intentions is to provide inspection agents, when carrying out their duties and under legal terms, with “portable recording devices, such as cameras”, similar to what has been happening in Hong Kong since August last year. This measure aims to ensure the legal protection of agents, “in situations of threat of physical conflict”. Recording devices must be used “visibly, attached to their uniform”, ensuring “always open and transparent” use and reserved for episodes in which agents “face threats or resistance during law enforcement”. Furthermore, agents will be obliged to inform the individual who will be the subject of recording in advance, before proceeding to capture images or sounds, and end it “as soon as the threats or resistance cease”. Furthermore, the portable recording device “will emit a visual notification, such as an indicator light, during the recording process”, allowing the person in question to be aware of the recordings, guaranteeing “the public's right to information and access to it”. The records will be stored “for the sole purpose of proving the occurrence of a threat or resistance during the law enforcement process”, and “all data will be deleted seven days after the date of recording, with the aim of guaranteeing the preservation of personal data in an adequate manner”, refer to the SSM. The consultation period began yesterday and will continue until April 8th, and the SSM will prepare the final report within 90 days after the end of the public consultation. Yesterday, a consultation session took place aimed at the industrial, commercial and tourist sectors. Another session is scheduled for March 14th, with civic associations, while a third will be held on March 19th, aimed at the public.

item.title: Predominance of executive power requires population and sectoral support
item.description:
Governing the special administrative regions based on the predominance of executive power “is not an easy task”, noted the Deputy Prime Minister, Ding Xuexiang, which is why he argued that it is not just a responsibility of the leaders of Macau and Hong Kong. Quoted by “Ou Mun Tin Toi”, the also member of the Standing Committee of the Politburo of the Communist Party of China argued that the predominance of the executive power requires the cooperation and support of all sectors of society, including the legislative and judicial powers. In a joint meeting with members from Hong Kong and Macao at the National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference, Ding Xuexiang emphasized that executive-led governance is not only the responsibility of the Chief Executive, but also of the SAR governments, the general public and all sectors of society. More than 200 people attended the meeting, including Xia Baolong, director of the State Council's SAR Affairs Office. On the occasion, Ding Xuexiang also stressed that the SARs must take advantage of the main opportunities presented by the 15th Five-Year Plan and take “solid measures” to promote “high-quality” economic development. He also asked to inspire more people in Hong Kong and Macau to “love both the country and its regions”, in addition to promoting the integration of the SARs into the country's overall development. In a meeting with deputies from the SAR to the National People's Assembly (ANP), the deputy PM proposed to “firmly promote the adequate diversification of the economy, further advance the construction of the Deep Cooperation Zone in Hengqin, and effectively leverage the boosted effect of industrial clusters”, as well as “continuously highlight the role of ‘a center, a platform and a base’”, according to Chinese radio. Praising the work of the ANP deputies, he also expressed hope that they will continue to perform their duties diligently and achieve results, fully supporting the SAR Government, “uniting all sectors of society and jointly promoting the prosperity and development of Hong Kong and Macau to a new level”.
Kou Hoi In talks about new objectives for the Executive In turn, Kou Hoi In, member of the ANP Standing Committee and former president of the Macau Legislative Assembly, stated that the 15th Five-Year Plan presented by the Central Government clearly defines new objectives for the Executives of the Macao SAR and Hong Kong. According to him, according to the new guidelines, in the coming years, Beijing wants a greater focus on proactive measures to increase government effectiveness. Therefore, the Macao SAR Executive must increase transparency and raise the level of professionalization to reinforce society's trust. Kou Hoi In also stressed that the territory must improve institutional coordination, invest in the training of qualified staff and effectively use digital and intelligent resources. Furthermore, he pointed out that integration in the construction of the Greater Bay Area and the advancement of the development of the Deep Cooperation Zone are two fundamental points for Macau to assume a more relevant role in the context of national development. On the other hand, Kou Hoi In argued that, in an international context marked by complexity and instability, the SARs have the advantage of being platforms for connecting with the world. In this sense, they can help to expand the country's international cooperation space and deepen cultural exchanges.
Chief reiterates that state security law is a constitutional duty The Chief Executive reiterated that the law relating to the defense of State security is a constitutional responsibility of Macau, in an interview with China News Service. Quoted by TDM, based on news from “Ou Mun Tin Toi”, Sam Hou Fai also considered that the stable and long-term application of the “one country, two systems” principle will depend on the legal mechanisms provided for in the new law. Stressing that national security legislation constitutes a constitutional duty and a prerequisite to ensure the stable and long-term implementation of the “one country, two systems” principle, Sam Hou Fai also argued that the provisions relating to the national security system, mechanisms and legal framework provide fundamental guarantees for the territory to respond to the complex external environment. Therefore, it considers that the constant improvement of the law is fundamental for the correct implementation of the Basic Law.
Economic diversification seen as “essential” for Macau’s future Leonel Alves, Macau's representative at the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference, told TDM that economic diversification is “essential” to “guarantee Macau's future as a special administrative region”. In Beijing for the “Two Sessions”, the Macanese addressed the topics in focus: “The world is very troubled, China is in full economic and social development, it has many objectives to achieve, the main topic that arises is the 15th Five-Year Plan and the inclusion of Macau in the country's development plan”. Leonel Alves stressed that these issues are relevant “for all communities in Macau”, because, he added, “China's progress also implies Macau's progress, China's stability is also Macau's stability”. Chan Hong, deputy for Macau to the National People's Assembly, defended TDM that the Macao SAR should make a greater contribution to national development.
CP.

item.title: Macao SAR bodies promise to implement “spirit” of the “Two Sessions”
item.description:
epa12795922 A general view of the opening ceremony of the Fourth Session of the 14th National People's Congress of China at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, China, 05 March 2026. China's major annual political meetings, known as the 'Lianghui' or 'Two Sessions,' began on 04 March with the opening of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), while the National People's Congress (NPC) opens on 05 March. EPA/ANDRES MARTINEZ CASARES
Following the “Two Sessions” in Beijing, the Macao SAR bodies promised to implement the “spirit” of the ANP and the CPPCC, highlighting above all the predominance of executive power and the safeguarding of national security for the stability of the territory
Macao SAR high-ranking officials promised to implement the “spirit” of the “Two Sessions” after the fourth session of the 14th National People's Assembly and the 14th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC). The president of the Court of Final Instance (TUI) began by saying that the persistence of the “one country, two systems” principle is the “fundamental basis and guarantee of long-term prosperity and stability” of Macau; Integration in the country represents “the inevitable direction for the future” of the territory. Song Man Lei also stressed that the judicial bodies and administrative bodies, although with different functions, “share the same fundamental objective, which is to guarantee the general interests of Macau society and the fundamental well-being of its residents”. In this sense, he said that the Macao SAR courts “will exercise judicial power in accordance with the law, guided by the concept of impartial justice, firmly supporting the Chief Executive in his governance (…) and actively cooperating, at the judicial level, with the Macao SAR Government in increasing the efficiency of governance”. In view of the 15th National Five-Year Plan and the 3rd Five-Year Plan of the Macao SAR, Song said that the courts of the three instances “will seriously study and implement the spirit of the ‘Two Sessions’”, focusing on four aspects. First of all, “persist in impartial justice and improve judicial efficiency”, and “firmly safeguard national security, severely punishing, in accordance with the law, crimes against national security”. Thirdly, the president of the TUI highlighted the reinforcement of the training of the team of judges, “cultivating the awareness of defending the sovereignty, security and development interests of the State, ensuring the unity of the political, legal and social effects of the judgment of cases”. Finally, he said that the judicial cooperation mechanism with Mainland China will be improved. Song Man Lei also stated that the courts will continue to safeguard the principle of “one country, two systems”, “cooperating with the various sectors of society in realizing the predominance of executive power”. The Prosecutor, Tong Hio Fong, said that the Public Ministry “will remain faithful to the political structure with the predominance of the executive branch, intensify communication with the executive and legislative bodies, carry out its functions in a fair and impartial manner and process processes rigorously and in compliance with the law”. He also guaranteed that he will increase staff awareness “regarding the inseparable link between national security and the stability and prosperity of the Macao SAR”, as well as “resolutely” combat “illegal and criminal acts that endanger the sovereignty, security and development interests of the State”. The president of the Legislative Assembly (AL), in turn, assured that the Hemicycle will “make every effort” to contribute “proactively” to the implementation of the 15th Five-Year Plan, namely by carrying out “scientific studies on priority work, presenting opinions and suggestions on policies and legislation”, so that there is a “scientific articulation” with the 3rd Plan of the Macao SAR. André Cheong also said that the AL “will take concrete measures to implement the principle of the predominance of executive power, so as not to be just a ‘commentator’ or ‘spectator’”. Specifically, through “internal reforms, it will seek to achieve high-quality political discussion and will make every effort to promote legislative production and supervision”. He also promised that the body he heads will “strengthen coordinated legislative production and promote the connection of rules and mechanisms between Macau and Hengqin”. The five Secretaries also stated that they will implement the “spirit” of the “Two Sessions”, stating that “the predominance of the executive power will be firmly defended”. Special mention goes to the Secretary for Security, who stated that “the safeguarding of national security and social stability constitute the common mission and responsibility of all security personnel”. In this sense, he considers that “it is imperative to understand and implement” the “spirit” of the ANP and the CPPCC. Chan Tsz King also discussed the legislative work on the laws and complementary diplomas relating to the State Security Defense Commission, which he considered “an important step to respond to and integrate the national development plan”. The Commissioners against Corruption and Audit, the director general of the Customs Services and the commander general of the Unitary Police Services also assured that they will study and learn the “spirit” of the “Two Sessions”.
CP.

item.title: New Yaohan will leave the Grand Lisboa Palace
item.description:
In early 2027, New Yaohan will vacate the commercial space it occupies at the Grand Lisboa Palace, paying nearly 32 million Hong Kong dollars to SJM Resorts to terminate the contract, the gaming operator announced, promising to “better align” the resort’s offering with “consumers’ ever-evolving preferences.” On the other hand, SJM renewed the agreement with STDM regarding the provision of various services, including transport, laundry and hotel management
SERGIO TERRA
SJM Resorts and NYH Gestão de Vendas a Retalho, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Sociedade de Turismo e Diversões de Macau (STDM), signed an agreement to terminate the contract for the right to use the commercial area on the second floor of the Grand Lisboa Palace resort, by the New Yaohan brand. As part of the termination, which takes effect from January 1, 2027, NYH will pay SJM Resorts around 31.9 million Hong Kong dollars (HKD) – including in cash, deposit compensation and vouchers redeemable at New Yaohan or Panda – and will have to return the space within 60 days, that is, in the first two months of next year. In a statement sent to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, SJM Resorts states its intention to “improve the resort’s integrated service offering, better aligning it with consumers’ ever-evolving preferences”. “This will serve as a catalyst to strengthen the global competitiveness of the Grand Lisboa Palace Resort in the COTAI market and will contribute positively to the value and long-term sustainability of its ecosystem”, he adds. According to data released on Thursday, Grand Lisboa Palace's EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, amortization and depreciation) earnings fell 66.9% to HKD 165 million in 2025, a decline that SJM attributed “to high levels of reinvestment”. SJM ended the 2025 financial year with net losses of 429 million, after having made a profit of 3 million in the previous year, and EBITDA gains of 3.2 billion (minus 15%), having justified the losses with the impact of the closure of satellite casinos and an “increasingly competitive environment”. SJM and NYH also decided to adjust the fees relating to the operation of the commercial area at the Grand Lisboa Palace in 2026. The minimum monthly base fee decreased from around 959 thousand to 492 thousand HKD, while the monthly percentage on store sales turnover rose from 4% to 5%. Until the end of this year, “NYH will only be able to introduce new fashion brands in the facilities with the prior written authorization of SJM Resorts”, indicates the same note. “The conclusion of the termination agreement will allow both SJM Resorts and New Yaohan sufficient time to carry out a smooth and orderly transition, with minimal disruption to their respective operations, while allowing for optimal use of the facilities”, highlighted the gaming operator.
Renewed service contract with STDM On the other hand, SJM announced the renewal of the service provision contract with STDM, which holds a majority stake of 54.8% in SJM Holdings. In 2026, SJM's ventures will continue to count on the support of the parent company in areas such as accommodation for gaming customers, promotion and advertising, food and beverages, transport, maintenance and engineering, laundry and hotel management. The agreement establishes the payment of a maximum of 96.6 million HKD in 2026 for accommodation services, 52.1% more than the 63.5 million agreed for 2025. The limit for promotional and advertising services – including shopping coupons for group customers – rose 42.9%, from 19.1 million to 27.3 million HKD. The annual limits were determined based on several factors, including, among others, “the historical consumption” of hotel accommodation and promotional and advertising services, “the projected number of casino patrons” of SJM, “the expected volume of visitor arrivals and gaming revenue in Macau in the coming years”, “the group’s business, marketing and promotional plans”, and “a safety margin of approximately 10% for inflation, business growth and unforeseen demand for services”. At the same time, the agreement on the “inheritance” of gaming chips issued by STDM was reviewed for three years until the end of the monopoly on casino operations. Since April 2002, SJM has used, as a loan, STDM tokens in its operations, with the parent company ensuring the repayment of the respective face value, which “substantially decreased in relation to the levels observed in previous years”. The new limit associated with the token contract amounts to HKD 75.8 million for 2026, 2027 and 2028, taking into account the approximate value of STDM tokens in circulation. In November 2025, SJM opened a new gaming space in Lisboa, with tables and slots moved from recently closed satellite casinos, which also made it possible to expand the capacity of the Grand Lisboa Palace. On the Peninsula, the Grand Lisboa and Lisboa hotels continue to be subject to modernization works, to improve the offer in the areas of gaming, hotels and restaurants.

item.title: “Now How?” will satirize the economic impact of the closure of satellite casinos
item.description:
The Dóci Papiaçám Group is already preparing its new theater show in Patuá. “Now How?” is the title of the play in the Creole language. It will be on stage at the Cultural Center, on the 23rd and 24th of May. The story presents as a backdrop the current panorama of the local economy, giving the example of the restaurant “Glorioso”, which stopped doing business after the closure of the satellite casinos. “There is a very big contrast between the favorable results of the economic recovery and the reality of people’s situation and the many stores that are closing,” the person responsible for the piece, Miguel de Senna Fernandes, told Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU
VÍTOR REBELO
“Agora Como?”, an expression widely used by Macanese people for 40 or more years, which comes from Cantonese and which means in Portuguese “And now?”, is the title of this year's show by Grupo Dóci Papiaçám di Macau. It will be on display in the Grand Auditorium of the Cultural Center, in May, on the 23rd, in the evening, and 24th, in the afternoon, as part of the Arts Festival program. The play presents as a backdrop the situation of the economy in the territory, especially after the closure of the satellite casinos. “It’s a very current topic,” the person responsible for producing the show in Patuá told Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU. Miguel de Senna Fernandes highlights that “there is a very big contrast between the favorable results of the economic recovery and the reality of people's situation and the many stores that are closing”. These “mixed feelings”, as the producer of the “show” refers, “are very much reflected in the way people are in Macau, in the results of the casinos, in the invasion of tourists”. “I have nothing against this, just that the income that results from this is not the best for the local population, who are naturally attracted by the best conditions outside the territory”, he points out. Admitting that “this has already become commonplace”, the person responsible then set out to develop the plot. “We are talking about a restaurant that is practically in shambles, close to going bankrupt, after many years considered a popular space and a reference in Portuguese and Macanese gastronomy, frequented by Portuguese, Chinese, nightlife people and mainly linked to gambling”, says the person in charge. To try to decide what to do, the two daughters of the now deceased owner “arrive” in Macau. Marta, coming from Portugal, played by Joana Freitas, and Elena, who lives in Spain, played by Agurtzane Aspiazu, do not have the same opinion when it comes to the solution to be given to the restaurant. Marta and Elena are the main protagonists of the play. “One wants to maintain it, because it is a historic place, and the other, more pragmatic, doubts its viability and therefore does not believe in the business due to the fact that people are now leaving Macau”, says the argument of the play. This is where Tiago Ramalho comes into play, played by João Picanço, a gastronomic consultant for an apparent company that is in charge of managing establishments in the area, since one of the candidates to receive a subsidy is the restaurant in question. “This whole situation is linked to the casinos, to the closure of the satellite casinos”, mentions Senna Fernandes, for whom “when comedy is done it is all ‘in extremis’, but in any case it reflects the current state of things, this is because, unfortunately, despite the economic recovery, there are a lot of stores closing and the satellite casinos are easy to close, because all it takes is a decree, but the consequences take a long time for people to get used to”. This “emptiness environment”, as the theater producer describes it, calls for “some kind of solutions and people have to live”. This year's patuá show will revolve around this theme. "After all, what do we want and what don't we want? Let's all go to the Mountain Island, to Hengqin?", he says, admitting that "it's our interpretation of things, our current picture". Hence the name of the play “Agora Como?”. As for the main focus of the story, the restaurant “Glorioso” (whose owner was a Belenenses fan…) does not close. “We want a ‘happy ending’, but it will be different”, maintains Miguel de Senna Fernandes.
Veteran actors provide great animation In this play there are some returns, such as Sharoz Pernencar (will play the character Anísio) and Alice Meira (Alda). “They haven't participated for several years and are now back”, highlights the person in charge. In total there will be around a dozen and a half people on stage. “It's the usual veteran crowd, who are always lively, used to these wanderings and then have that ability to make the story happen, even if they don't have much to say there is a dynamic that is behind it all and that animates the story”, he emphasizes. In recent times, the backstage staff has been decreasing. “Our scenarios have also been simplified, in the sense that they don’t require much intervention, they don’t need as many people”, he mentions. The production team will make use of what technology allows them to do. “We will use everything that is accessible to us and that fits into the show, always with the guidance of Sérgio Perez”, notes Senna Fernandes, highlighting that the preparation is still in the script reading phase, later moving on to rehearsals in the D. José da Costa Nunes Kindergarten gymnasium. The Macanese Creole-language show has been making audiences laugh since 1994, always with the same director. This will be the 31st, as it was not held in 1998 and 2020. “It continues to give me a lot of pleasure doing the show, but I'm starting to think that young people are needed to take on the production, for other people to write the play, because deep down it was important that there was another view on things, I have mine and we often fall into that stratified situation”, he emphasizes. He recognizes that there may be “some stop in time”, so “if there are other people who want to look at this with different eyes and really immerse themselves in this art of conceiving and directing the play, they will have my full support and will count on me behind them, until the day they gain total independence”. As long as there are no alternatives, Miguel de Senna Fernandes will “write with gusto”, but insists: “It would be good to have new comedy solutions, as times change, people change and perhaps they are hungry for another type of comedy”. Recalling that the theater in Patuá has been on the list of China's intangible heritage since 2021, he states that “this is not just an honorific title, it has implications, it has duties, and the duties are to develop heritage transmission activities”. He notes that “transmission means succession, therefore, I look at this very carefully, because it is also a sign of the vitality of the community itself”. Regarding the evolution that has occurred over the years, the main producer says that the company is much more mature. “There is at least the conception of the show, the idea, the image, the philosophy of how it is done, people already have a much more mature notion, of course there is always one or another aspect that needs training”, he admits. Seeing the room full every year gives “encouragement” to the entire team, who consider themselves like a family. “It can only be like that”, he says. “I can't conceive of an amateur comedy theater in any other way, because if we don't enjoy the hilarious situation in rehearsals it won't work, there is no miracle for a good show”, he notes. “Even in situations where the show didn't go well, people didn't smile, and we've already reached this point, having less positive experiences, we had fun, which is the fundamental thing, and I can't imagine this family spirit, of mutual help among us who are amateurs, any other way”, he concludes.

item.title: SJM financed Santa Casa baskets again
item.description:
On Saturday, the Santa Casa da Misericórdia Social Store carried out the monthly distribution of food baskets, covering 372 families in need, including 150 from the Federation of Workers' Associations, 140 from the General Union of Residents' Associations of Macau, 25 from the Association of Family Carers of the Mentally Disabled and 47 directly supported by Santa Casa.
SJM contributed 300,000 patacas, a donation given by Daisy Ho, the company's executive administrator, to the Santa Casa provider, António José de Freitas. On the occasion, the person responsible said that SJM Resorts “maintains a strong social commitment, dedicating itself to supporting the most vulnerable groups”. This is the 14th consecutive year of supporting the initiative. Daisy Ho expressed satisfaction at “collaborating with this charity with centuries of history, jointly promoting charitable works in Macau, caring for those in need and fostering social harmony and stability”, highlighting that, since 2013, SJM has participated in and supported the Santa Casa social store, “promoting the spirit of solidarity and inclusion”. In addition to the baskets, red New Year envelopes were distributed to each family, conveying festive greetings, “in the hope of, through concrete actions, giving back to society and transmitting positive energy to the most disadvantaged groups in Macau”, highlighted Daisy Ho. António José de Freitas agradeceu o apoio contínuo da SJM desde o início do funcionamento da Loja Social e expressou também gratidão a outras empresas de lazer, diversos sectores da sociedade e entidades comerciais que, “ao longo dos anos, têm mantido o seu apoio inabalável, cumprindo a sua responsabilidade social e unindo esforços com dedicação e generosidade”, salientou o Provedor. The SJM Resorts team of volunteers mobilized more than 40 volunteers to help distribute the hampers.
V.R.

item.title: Emotional support mechanism helped more than 500 pregnant women
item.description:
More than 500 women received emotional support and monitoring, under the “Pilot project for a support mechanism for pregnant and postpartum women”, in the last three months of last year. Recently, the “Full Cycle Health Protection Plan for Pregnant Women and Postpartum Women” was launched, within the scope of which an “Open Emotional Support Line” will be created.
In the last quarter of last year, the “Pilot project for a support mechanism for pregnant and postpartum women” provided assessments, monitoring and emotional support to more than 500 women, according to the Health Services (SSM) and the Institute of Social Action (IAS). The assessment came about in connection with the launch of the “Complete Cycle Health Protection Plan for Pregnant Women and Postpartum Women”, which covers the physical, mental and social and family aspects and provides for the establishment of an “Open Emotional Support Line”. The plan ensures that couples who are about to become parents have access to “appropriate and timely assistance” through early screenings, health education, psychological assessments and support. The launch of the plan is “synonymous with a great integration and improvement of health care services” for women and children in the Macao SAR and an effort by the Government to “materialize the ‘Happy Macau’ government vision”, the statement read. During the launch ceremony, the director of the SSM, Alvis Lo, highlighted the Executive's intention to further strengthen cooperation between the health and social services sectors, uniting associations and institutions in order to transmit professional knowledge to the community and families. The plan was developed jointly between Centro Hospitalar Conde de São Januário, Macau Union Medical Center, Kiang Wu Hospital, community health centers and seven association groups. The associations involved include the General Women's Association of Macau, the General Union of Residents' Associations, the Federation of Workers' Associations, Caritas, the Methodist Church, the Salvation Army Association and the Youth Development Center. On the occasion, Hon Wai, president of the IAS, awarded certificates of appreciation to 13 integrated family and community support service centers for providing “professional services, discreetly, to pregnant women, postpartum women and families, in the long term”. Lao Kam Leng, president of the General Women's Association, thanked the Government, on behalf of the 13 distinguished centers, “for the constant support and encouragement of work to support pregnant and postpartum women”. Lao said that “social support institutions have permanently followed the three-tier model of ‘Prevention, Assistance and Reparation’ proposed by the IAS, with the aim of jointly creating a network of family and community services.” In the future, the Government foresees a deepening of cooperation, which will allow it to offer monitoring services for “every family with newborns”.
P.M.

item.title: DST takes advantage of fairs in Europe to attract and promote multi-destination itineraries
item.description:
The focus on attracting European tourists took DST to the Lisbon Tourism Exchange and the Berlin International Fair. In the Portuguese capital, the Macau delegation organized a seminar where they presented the territory's latest tourism products. In Germany, the campaign focused mainly on promoting gastronomy
VÍTOR REBELO
Tourism Services (DST) returned to Europe, being present at two fairs considered renowned in the sector, the Lisbon Tourism Exchange (BTL) and the Berlin International Tourism Fair (ITB), in Germany. In the Portuguese capital, for five days, DST installed a stand and carried out a series of promotional activities, taking advantage of Portugal's largest tourism fair to present Macau's tourist resources to European tour operators. During the event, the organization also held a promotion seminar, where it disclosed the latest innovations in Macau's tourism products, and representatives from several companies presented their products and services. DST also cooperated with Rádio Renascença de Portugal, with one of its program presenters, traveling to Macau before the fair, to publicize the tourist charm of the destination through live broadcasts of the radio program and content on social media. Together with the Portuguese Association of Travel and Tourism Agencies (APAVT), an online training course was launched to help European tour operators deepen their knowledge of Macau's “tourism +”, last year's edition of which attracted the participation of several tour operators. This year, in addition to the Portuguese edition, the course will have a German version, targeting the market in German-speaking countries, and one in English for the Finnish and Dutch markets. On the other hand, DST is carrying out a survey to collect and analyze the preferences of European visitors, to understand their intention to visit Macau on multi-destination itineraries, and better plan their market development strategy. After attending BTL, the DST delegation, which included director Maria Helena de Senna Fernandes, headed to Berlin, the city that organized another edition of the International Tourism Fair (ITB) for three days. In addition to promoting elements of “tourism +” in Macau, MGTO sought to attract European visitors on multi-destination itineraries and reinforce the image and influence of the MSAR in the international market, aiming to “expand European markets and other sources of long-haul international visitors”, it highlights in a statement, adding that, under the theme “Feel Macau”, the stand “showcased the rich and diverse elements of “tourism +” in Macau and its unique attractiveness of cultural exchange between East and West”. In cooperation with a cultural and artistic group from Macau, through creative installations and image content, the space showcased the characteristics of the Red Market, showing the image of community tourism. It also organized lion dance shows, Portuguese tile painting workshops and typical Macanese snacks, in order to make European professionals feel the “charm” of Macau’s intangible cultural heritage. During ITB, the China pavilion, together with the Macao SAR and Hong Kong stands, organized a sharing activity on social platforms, “reinforcing online and in-person promotion, with a view to expanding the multi-destination tourism market”, highlights DST. On the other hand, the themed tourist promotion action “Chinese Night and Flavors of China” took place at night, with Macau and several provinces and cities in Mainland China being the main points of promotion for the event. MGTO promoted Macau's gastronomy and tourism resources through video, presentation and interactive exhibitions, as part of the first of the 2026 “Nihao! China” international promotion activities. During the event, Maria Helena de Senna Fernandes presented Macau's unique gastronomic culture and cultural heritage, promoting its profile as a UNESCO Creative City in Gastronomy. In the space dedicated to the Macao SAR, lion dance shows, alua tasting, almond cookies and crispy chicken biscuits, among others, were also held. MGTO also presented the characteristics of Macau's markets and provided related information to inform guests, representatives of tourism companies from Mainland China and abroad, and the German press, about Macau's intangible cultural heritage and community tourism elements.

item.title: Lisbon-Hong Kong route lacks state support
item.description:
Business and aviation sector representatives warn that an air link between Lisbon and Hong Kong would have high costs and could require state support to reduce risk. This possible air connection has once again gained prominence following recent contacts between Portuguese authorities and the Hong Kong Airport Authority. At the beginning of February, a meeting took place between the consul-general of Portugal in Macau and Hong Kong, Alexandre Leitão, and the executive director of the Hong Kong Airport Authority (AAHK), Vivian Cheung. “[The meeting in Hong Kong was an] opportunity to discuss matters of mutual interest”, said the Consulate at the time. In a written response to questions from the Lusa agency, AAHK said it has sought to “establish contacts with airlines and commercial partners in the global sector, including government authorities and airport operators”. According to Erik Young, an aviation expert based in Hong Kong, a carrier like TAP would have to look “beyond the simple interests of passengers” and analyze several “critical pillars”. The consultant highlighted that “it would be necessary to assess the balance between high-performance business trips, tourism and, crucial for this type of long-distance journey”, the cargo capacity in the hold. “A flight like this does not just serve Hong Kong; its success depends on the Greater Bay Area catchment area and the efficiency with which the Lisbon hub can connect passengers to secondary markets in Brazil and Africa,” he said. In economic terms, he highlighted that “trends in direct foreign investment, trade volumes between Greater China and Portuguese-speaking markets” should be observed, and the relative competitiveness of TAP's fleet compared to carriers that offer one-stop connections. Young further pointed out that for long-haul routes with high entry costs, “some type of upfront support or a robust Air Services Agreement” is often the decisive factor in mitigating risk. "Ultimately, this is not a yes or no question. This is an in-depth feasibility project and not a quick observation. The business case requires a very specific alignment of these points", he concluded. The secretary general of the Luso-Chinese Chamber of Commerce and Industry (CCILC) told Lusa that the possibility of a direct air connection between Portugal and Hong Kong or Macau "arises cyclically in the public debate due to the growing historical and economic interest between Portugal and southern China. "From a technical point of view, today this connection appears to be possible", said Bernardo Mendia, remembering that the distance between Lisbon and Hong Kong is around 11 thousand kilometers and that modern long-haul aircraft, such as the Airbus TAP Air Portugal's A330-900neo has enough autonomy to carry out the non-stop flight. However, he highlighted that “the central issue is not technological, but above all economic” as “intercontinental routes require a consistent critical mass of passengers and cargo to be sustainable”. Currently, the market between Portugal and southern China is served indirectly through large European and Middle Eastern hubs. "There are more than 300 weekly flights between the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area and Lisbon with intermediate connections at airports such as Paris, Frankfurt, Istanbul or Dubai. This shows that there is demand, but it is still distributed across these large hubs", he added. Despite this, Mendia considers that the strategic potential is significant, considering that Greater Bay has more than 70 million inhabitants and constitutes one of the largest economic hubs in the world. The Lusophone space brings together around 260 million people, which creates a very relevant potential axis between Asia and Portuguese-speaking countries, he warns, in which “Portugal can play a natural role as a connecting platform”. In this context, a direct connection could benefit not only from traffic between Portugal and southern China, but also from broader flows between Asia and Portuguese-speaking markets. “Lisbon can take advantage of its geographical position and TAP Air Portugal’s Atlantic network,” he said. The CCILC secretary general also pointed out a “particularly interesting” scenario of the possible involvement of a Chinese airline in the TAP privatization process. “In this case, the possibility of positioning Lisbon as a true connecting hub between Europe, Asia and the Portuguese-speaking space could open up”, he stated. “Instead of Portuguese passengers having to travel to major European capitals to fly to Asia, the opposite could happen: passengers from several European cities start to use Lisbon as a gateway for direct flights to Asia, with all the associated benefits for Portugal”, he added. If this were to happen, concluded Mendia, “Lisbon could assert itself as a natural meeting point between three great economic spaces: Europe, the Greater Bay Area of ​​southern China and the Portuguese-speaking world”.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: Dragon Boat Regattas will feature 186 teams
item.description:
This year's edition of the Macau International Dragon Boat Regattas will take place in June, on the 13th, 14th and 19th. The organization expects the participation of 186 teams in the different categories, distributed between small and large boats
The Praia Grande Nautical Center will once again be the stage for the International Dragon Boat Regattas, this year scheduled for the month of June, namely on the 13th, 14th and 19th. The races, organized by the Sports Institute (ID), SJM Resorts and the Dragon Boat Association of Macau-China, and co-organized by the Institute for Municipal Affairs and the Administration and Public Service Services, maintain the same format, including a mixed race, after the debut in 2025. The races will take place during the Dragon Boat Festival, starting with the local competitions for Small Boats (the 13th), followed by the local races for Large Boats (the 14th) and the International Invitational Regattas for Large Boats and the University Regatta (the 19th). This year, limited places will once again be opened for local races, with an estimated 186 teams participating, distributed as follows: Macau Small Boat Regatta (60 in the Open Category and 24 in the Women's Category), Public Service Small Boat Regatta (18 teams), University Regatta for Small Boats (12 in the Open Category) and Large Boat Regatta (36 in the Category Open, 18 Women and 18 Mixed). The team registration period ended on Saturday, after a period in which associations or organizations had to register their teams, and from today onwards they can register online, which will run until 10pm on March 15th. Successfully registered teams must send the relevant information, including the list of crew members, by 10pm on April 24th. Requests to change elements must be submitted by May 6th. The organization will give registration priority to the 12 best teams in the Open Category and the six best in the Women's Category from last year's Regatta, as well as the six best in the Open Category and the best three in the Women's Category. The training period will begin on March 18th. The details of the competition were presented at a press conference, during which the deputy president of the ID, Lei Si Leng, stated that, “as an important annual international sporting event in Macau, the Regattas not only boost the development of this sport, but also promote the culture of this traditional Chinese festival, celebrating the Dragon Boat Festival with residents and tourists”. Meanwhile, the managing director of SJM, Daisy Ho, recalled that the company has supported and participated in dragon boat competitions for more than six decades, being “committed to promoting sport in Macau and the mission of preserving this intangible Chinese cultural heritage”. “Through this traditional sport solidly established in the region, we hope to deepen cultural and sporting exchanges throughout the Greater Bay Area and further consolidate Macau’s position as an international city of major sporting events”, he highlighted.
V.R.

item.title: Guy Delisle sees Comics as an “effective means” of journalism
item.description:
Award-winning author Guy Delisle, known for graphic non-fiction works, described comics as an “effective medium” of journalism that reaches a wider audience. The French-Canadian writer was a guest at the 15th edition of the Macau Literary Festival – Rota das Letras, which takes place between the 5th and 15th of March, for a conversation about a medium that “used to be seen as something just for children”, but which is nowadays read “by both grandparents and grandchildren”. Delisle has written several acclaimed books in the graphic nonfiction genre focusing on his travel and life experiences including Pyongyang, Jerusalem: Chronicles of the Holy City, Chronicles of Burma, and Shenzhen. However, the Quebec-born author considers himself more part of the first wave of graphic “travel book authors”, and less a journalist in the style of authors like Joe Sacco, known for books describing daily life in wartime settings in Palestine and the Balkans. "There are many directions, there are many travel comic books. Joe Sacco is a journalist and does something similar, but he has experience as a journalist and has a different perspective and approach", said Delisle in response to a question from Lusa. “I do some research into the context of the locations in focus, but I prefer the approach of making casual observations, pointing out the details that I find most funny and interesting,” he said. The author worked mainly in the animation industry, but ended up entering the world of graphic novels after being posted to Shenzhen in 1997. Starting as a method of telling his travel experiences “for the family”, turning his daily notes in this city in southern China into his first comic. The author would be transferred for work, unusually, to the capital of North Korea in 2001, which generated his most translated book, Pyongyang. "I was sent to North Korea for two months. I knew a lot of people from the animation industry who told me it was super boring [...] but I always felt fascinated by the country", he highlighted. "When I got there I received flowers to put at the feet of a statue of Kim Jong Il [leader of North Korea at the time]. That alone made me think it would make a good book." The writer also received the top prize for European Comics when the French edition of “Jerusalém” was chosen Best Album at the Angoulême International Comic Festival. One of his most journalistic works includes “Hostage”, which describes the experience of Christophe Andre, a member of Doctors Without Borders, kidnapped for three months as a hostage in Chechnya. “A kidnapping is a very difficult experience, but when I asked him he told me everything”, he recalled. Used to describing his memories, Delisle had to describe the experiences of others for the first time, making “almost 8-hour recordings” of conversations with Andre. “It was very good material, but the first version felt more like a Hollywood film and it worked, so I changed the style to a realistic depiction of the experience of someone deprived of their freedom.” The book describes, for example, how at one point the kidnappers forgot to close the bedroom door, with Andre pondering for hours whether he should risk leaving. "He waited a few hours until it was dark. The question I ask the reader is what would he do in his situation? Would he open the door?", said Delisle. His most recent book, MuyBridge published in 2024, describes the life of Eadeward MuyBridge, one of the pioneers of photography, with Delisle revealing that a new book will be published “next week”. As for using his time in Macau and Hong Kong for a book, he considers that “unfortunately he would need to stay much longer” than three months to have enough notes for a book. This year's edition of the Macau Literary Festival pays tribute to Camilo Pessanha, on the centenary of the Portuguese poet's death. The festival program also includes journalist and political commentator João Miguel Tavares, who published the book “José Sócrates – Ascensão” in October. Also coming to Macau is Miguel Carvalho, author of “Por Dentro do Chega. The hidden face of the extreme right in Portugal”, which is the result of an investigation that the journalist and writer carried out over five years.
Rodrigo Leão concert postponed due to impact of war on aviation Rodrigo Leão's concert in Macau, scheduled for Wednesday, was postponed due to the impact of the war in Iran on air connections, and should take place “in April or later”, the organization told Lusa. The show “O Rapaz da Montanha” is part of the 15th edition of the Rota das Letras literary festival. "We have to see when the large auditorium room at the Macau Cultural Center will be available again, in March I already know that it is impossible. At the very least it will be in April or later, but we will try to make it in April", said the festival director. Ricardo Pinto noted that it is necessary to reconcile the availability of the musician and the team, the venue, in addition to “seeing if plane prices have now normalized”. “They were asking for 30 thousand patacas or 40 thousand patacas, at least, for each flight in economy class”, he said. In a statement, released on social media, the festival explained that, “despite the best efforts of the production team to guarantee alternative routes, the cancellation of origin air connections made it impossible to arrive in Macau as planned”, it reads. According to the same note, tickets already purchased remain valid, although a full refund of the ticket is possible for those who cannot attend the new date to be announced.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: The only language in the world created by women is still hope for gender equality
item.description:
Nüshu is a script born of female exclusion in southern China, created by women and unique in the world, and survives through young people who see in the ancient traces a message of hope for gender equality. Pan Shengwen is very young, just 21 years old, but she has spent the last four years immersed in this ancient script, becoming one of the few people alive who can read and write it fluently. Her mission, she tells Lusa with calm determination, is to ensure that the world understands the “power of women” and to take the message of gender equality to a new generation through this writing that women created when they had no voice. Nüshu means “women's writing” in Chinese, and is the only exclusively female writing system discovered anywhere in the world, passed down from generation to generation by the women of Jiangyong County, a remote, mountainous region in southern Hunan province. The history of Nüshu dates back centuries, to a time when Chinese women did not have access to formal education. “Girls could only peek into the classrooms from outside, while boys learned to write,” explains Pan. This circumstance was decisive in the development of writing. “When you look at Chinese square characters from the side, they take the shape of willow leaves”, they are transformed when viewed from a certain angle into thin, elongated shapes, he describes. “They didn’t have ink or brushes, so they used sticks and ashes, and wrote anywhere, on fans, on fabric, on everything they could find”, says the young woman. In an era where education was a male privilege, Nüshu became something much deeper than mere communication. It was women’s “only spiritual refuge,” a private domain where they could explore ideas, express emotions and connect with each other beyond the watchful eyes of patriarchal society, she explains. "They used Nüshu to compose poetry, translate stories and write letters, channeling talents and aspirations through it. When no path was open to them, they forged a new one", he says. “The creation and use of Nüshu embody women’s efforts to guarantee the right to expression in an extremely unequal social environment,” he adds. The culture of Nüshu embodies a very rich “spirit of female solidarity”, being transmitted exclusively between mothers and daughters, and between sisters, within the strict core of the female community. “In Nüshu culture, I feel something profound, the tolerance, understanding and admiration that women show for each other,” reflects Pan. “Whether it is between mothers and daughters, sisters, or even women who have never met, this mutual support is incredibly powerful.” This brotherhood was essential to Nüshu's survival. Women embroidered the characters onto fabric, integrating them into their daily lives, and when a woman died her writings in Nüshu were burned. This traditional practice led to the near extinction of writing, with most of its history lost in fire and ashes. The oldest surviving works cannot be dated with certainty. “In feudal times, Nüshu was a culture of suffering, a way for women to express their inner anguish,” explains Pan. “But in modern society, it should be interpreted as a positive and uplifting culture,” he adds. Pan Shengwen defines the spirit of Nüshu as “feminine” or “feminist” culture, rather than resistance. “It is inclusive and expansive enough,” he says. “It is by no means limited to resistance.” The young woman believes that contemporary Chinese women are demonstrating “increasing strength and capacity in all fields” and what makes Nüshu resonate in the new era is their “indomitable vitality”, she describes. Despite his age, Pan founded the “Nüshu Public Art Development Project” and launched collaborative public art initiatives in universities and communities across China. The project uses artistic expression to raise funds that support two groups of women particularly dear to the young woman: mothers fighting cancer and needy girls in mountainous regions. “When I felt tempted to give up, many girls in the project told me: ‘You’re doing an incredible job, you inspired us’”, Pan Shengwen tells Lusa. “This strength that we women give to each other made me realize that Nüshu could become my life mission”. Pan Shengwen was in Macau this Sunday, International Women's Day, to lead the workshop “Nushu, learning to write the language of women”, which will be followed by the screening of the documentary “Unfathomable Characters, female writing as an act of resistance”, by Violet Du Feng. The events are part of the Rota das Letras Festival, which takes place in the city, in the Casa Garden auditorium.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: Agreement on medical exercise in China subject to new consultation
item.description: The Directorate of Economic and Technological Development Services is once again carrying out consultations on the amendment to the “Agreement on Trade in Services under CEPA”. In this sense, the Health Services (SSM) hope that “the opinions of the sectors involved can be heard, creating more convenient conditions and opportunities for the exercise of the activity of health professionals in Mainland China”. The 19th plenary session of the Council for Medical Affairs discussed the consultation opinions on the amendment to the Agreement, which allows Macau doctors and pharmacists to register for national qualification examinations. In July 2023, the Council held an extended meeting to gather opinions, the contents of which include, among other things, allowing permanent residents who have obtained a bachelor's degree in the specialty of general medicine and nursing to participate in the Mainland China doctor qualification examination. The Council meeting, chaired by Alvis Lo, director of the SSM, also discussed the work to deepen the “Healthy Community” program. Through interdepartmental collaboration and coordination with associations from different sectors, the program focuses on promoting healthy behaviors and proactive health management, in order to comprehensively achieve the objectives defined in the “Action Plan for Healthy Macau”. The community health consultation posts launched under the program have already attracted more than 140,000 participants.

item.title: Chinese parents use social media to find sons-in-law and daughters-in-law
item.description:
Chinese parents are turning to social media to find sons-in-law and daughters-in-law, through advertisements and discussions focused on practical attributes and marked by a notable “absence of love”, reveals research by an academic from the University of Macau (UM). The study by Todd Lyle Sandel, professor in the Communications department at UM, focuses on parental involvement in dating groups on the WeChat messaging platform, where parents often describe their children as if they were listing products on a market: “Height, weight, education, economic situation, home ownership” are the typical criteria presented, the researcher revealed this Friday in the presentation of the work, which is still ongoing. “What is striking is the absence of ‘love’ or ‘feeling’ in the presentation ads,” Sandel added, the focus is on measurable characteristics, not emotional connection, he told a conference at UM. “Men marry downwards, women marry upwards”, with the expectation of “men as providers and women as housewives”, with the dominant preference being that a “woman marries an older man who has a house, a higher income and is highly educated”, the investigation points out. This practice mirrors the physical marriage markets still found today in parks in many Chinese cities, where parents display slips of paper with details about the age, education and profession of potential spouses to their sons and daughters. Sandel's analysis is based on observation, starting in 2024, of a WeChat group with approximately 500 members dedicated to parents finding partners for their children, functioning as a digital version of traditional marriage markets in parks. In one of the advertisements collected by the researcher, a mother writes about her daughter: “Good natured, slender figure, fair skin, normally does not wear makeup, appears to be much younger than her real age”. You want a man “taller than 170 cm, stable job, who works in Guangzhou, is not bald, is not fat, has a calm temperament, is responsible, honest, preferably does not smoke or drinks little and shares domestic tasks”. The description of the boys by their parents emphasizes material and moral assets: “only child, retired parents, has his own house, is ambitious, has a driving license, respects his elders”. From Sandel's investigation, two distinct but interconnected sociological realities come to the fore: the difficulties faced by highly successful women, at the top of the social pyramid, the so-called “surplus women”; and that of men from the lower classes, especially in rural areas. “Women with high levels of education and income have difficulty finding a partner because the traditional paradigm requires the man to be ‘superior’”, explained the researcher. “At the top of the pyramid, there are few men who meet this criteria,” he added. Conversely, Todd Lyle Sandel noted that men in rural areas face a shortage of women, a situation driven by the still-existing imbalance in gender ratios and the migration of women to urban centers. The average age at first marriage, according to Sandel, according to data from 2020, is on average 29.54 years for men and 28.14 years for women, with a trend towards increasing age in both cases, increasing year after year. Finally, according to the results of Sandel's investigation so far, the WeChat group's observation suggests that successfully meeting a son-in-law or daughter-in-law through these channels "is rare."
JTM with Lusa
